zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaretha van Brabant於1192年出生在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文時，她的父親Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen32歲，母親Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace30歲。她於1207年與Gerhard III. von Geldern在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1231年9月21日在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德享年39歲，埋葬在荷蘭利包格洛爾蒙德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaretha van Brabant于1192年出生在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文时，她的父亲Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen32岁，母亲Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace30岁。她于1207年与Gerhard III. von Geldern在荷兰蓋爾德藍德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1231年9月21日在荷兰蓋爾德藍德享年39岁，埋葬在荷兰利包格洛爾蒙德。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Margaretha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margarethe
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.description: Roermond has been a city of historical importance. He received his municipal liberties in 1231 and was a member of the Hanseatic League.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaretha van Brabant was born in 1192, in Leuven, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, her father, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, was 32 and her mother, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, was 30. She married Gerhard III. von Geldern in 1207, in Zutphen, Gelderland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 21 September 1231, in Gelderland, Netherlands, at the age of 39, and was buried in Roermond, Limburg, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaretha van Brabant est née en 1192, à Leuven, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, son père, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Gerhard III. von Geldern en 1207, à Zutphen, Gueldre, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 septembre 1231, à Gueldre, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Roermond, Limbourg, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaretha van Brabant 1192 in Leuven, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen 32 und ihre Mutter Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace 30. Sie hat 1207 in Zutphen, Geldern, Niederlande Gerhard III. von Geldern geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 21. September 1231 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Geldern, Niederlande und wurde in Roermond, Limburg, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaretha van Brabant è nata il nel 1192 nel Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, sua padre, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Gerhard III. von Geldern nel 1207, nel Zutphen, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 21 settembre 1231, nel Gelderland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolta a Roermond, Limburgo, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaretha van Brabantが1192年にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントレウベンで生まれた時，父Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingenは32歳，母Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsaceは30歳であった。1207年にオランダジェルダーランドズトフンでGerhard III. von Geldernと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1231年9月21日にオランダジェルダーランドで39歳で死去し，オランダリンブルグロールモンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaretha van Brabant(이)가 1192년 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 레우븐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1207년 네덜란드 글델랜드 주트프에서 Gerhard III. von Geldern(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1231년 9월 21일 네덜란드 글델랜드에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 림버그 로몽에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaretha van Brabant nasceu em 1192, em Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, seu pai, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Gerhard III. von Geldern em 1207, em Zutphen, Gheldria, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 21 de setembro de 1231, em Gheldria, Países Baixos, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Roermond, Limburgo, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaretha van Brabant родилась в 1192 г. в местности леувен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerhard III. von Geldern в 1207 г. в местности зутфен, джелдирланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 21 сентября 1231 г. в местности джелдирланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности роирмонд, лимбург, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaretha van Brabant nació en 1192, en Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, su papá, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Gerhard III. von Geldern en 1207, en Zutphen, Gelderland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 21 de septiembre de 1231, en Gelderland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Roermond, Limburg, Países Bajos.
